content.body: This document exists to define how UX JSON should remain compatible with document databases and admin UI workflows. Use it when shaping IDs, collections, split files, mappings, imports, exports, or comparison views.

content.title: Storage Principle
content.body: The schema should work as file-based JSON and as document-based database records. Collection files are preferred for repo authoring, but every item must be self-contained enough to become a DB document.

content.title: Recommended Collections
body: Use these logical collections when seeding a database or powering an admin UI:

The v1 schema defines the core UX hierarchy in detail: package manifests, coverage indexes, feature, subfeature, journey, flow, subflow, requirement, observable scenario, acceptance criterion, pending decision, and current-to-desired mapping objects.

content.title: Stable IDs
body: - globally unique within the repo or corpus namespace

content.title: Admin UI Views
body: An admin UI should be able to render:
Mappings should drive comparison views rather than requiring current and desired rows to know about each other directly.

content.title: Split And Compose
body: Scripts may split a collection into one file per item or compose item files into a collection. This must not change IDs, status, authority, mode, owner, scope, or mapping meaning.
When composing, preserve stable order by explicit order fields when present, then by ID.

content.title: Versioning
content.body: Store schema versions with every artifact and item. Future migrations should be explicit and scriptable, and should not silently rewrite governing behavior.
